Title: Transcriptome-referenced association study provides insights into the regulation of oil and fatty acid biosynthesis in Torreya grandis kernel

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Surfeit locus protein 1 family; cytochrome c oxidase assembly-associated protein
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: May indirectly promote/regulate sciadonic acid biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression level positively and significantly associated with sciadonic acid content
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: kernel transcript abundance positively correlated with sciadonic acid content across landraces
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: cytochrome c oxidase assembly machinery; cytochrome c oxidase (inferred from annotation/discussion)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TRAS association; Pearson correlation analysis of transcript expression versus sciadonic acid content
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SNP15 in BRD_TGR26378 was significantly associated with sciadonic acid. 2. BRD_TGR26378 was annotated as SURF1 and its expression positively correlated with sciadonic acid content. 3. The paper proposes TgSURF1 may indirectly regulate sciadonic acid biosynthesis, likely via cytochrome c oxidase-related processes.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate marker for sciadonic acid improvement, but requires direct functional validation before deployment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Role is indirect and inferred; no transgenic or biochemical validation was performed in this study.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression positively and significantly associated with arachidic acid content
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: kernel transcript abundance positively correlated with arachidic acid content across landraces
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SNP1 in BRD_TGR74645 was significantly associated with arachidic acid. 2. The transcript was annotated as acyl-CoA thioesterase. 3. Its expression positively correlated with arachidic acid content.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker for arachidic acid-associated variation; not directly validated
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as associated transcript, but not functionally validated beyond association/correlation.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression positively and significantly associated with palmitic acid content
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: kernel transcript abundance positively correlated with palmitic acid content across landraces
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SNP12 in BRD_TGR17081 was significantly associated with palmitic acid. 2. The transcript was annotated as probable arabinosyltransferase. 3. Its expression positively correlated with palmitic acid content.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker for palmitic acid-associated variation; requires direct validation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely indirect role; annotation suggests cell wall organization rather than direct lipid metabolism.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression positively and significantly associated with eicosenoic acid content
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: kernel transcript abundance positively correlated with eicosenoic acid content across landraces
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SNP4 in BRD_TGR57892 was significantly associated with eicosenoic acid. 2. Expression of BRD_TGR57892 positively correlated with eicosenoic acid content. 3. Functional identity remains unknown.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker for eicosenoic acid-associated variation, pending gene identification

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression positively and significantly associated with eicosenoic acid content
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: kernel transcript abundance positively correlated with eicosenoic acid content across landraces
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SNP3 in BRD_TGR20025 was significantly associated with eicosenoic acid. 2. Expression of BRD_TGR20025 positively correlated with eicosenoic acid content. 3. Functional identity remains unknown.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker for eicosenoic acid-associated variation, pending functional annotation

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 170 diverse landraces; transient leaf assay system
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl group from acyl-CoA to diacylglycerol to form triacylglycerol
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression positively correlated with TgLBD40 in kernel transcriptomes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter tested in tobacco leaves; transcript level correlated with TgLBD40 across T. grandis landraces
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; Pearson correlation analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TgDGAT1 is an oil biosynthesis pathway gene positively associated with TgLBD40 expression. 2. TgLBD40 directly binds the TgDGAT1 promoter. 3. TgLBD40 enhances TgDGAT1 promoter activity only when combined with TgWRI1, linking TgDGAT1 to the LBD40-mediated oil accumulation mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering increased kernel oil through pathway enhancement, especially in combination with regulators such as TgLBD40 and TgWRI1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes oil accumulation as a downstream TAG biosynthetic enzyme target
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TgDGAT1 native promoter used as reporter in dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not identified by TRAS as core associated transcript for trait, but experimentally validated as a direct downstream metabolic target of the core regulator TgLBD40.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Torreya grandis; Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 170 diverse landraces; Col-0; atlbd41 mutant Salk_078678C and SALK_144556C; tobacco transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes oil accumulation and increases seed oil content
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: kernel; seed; leaf in transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation in combination with TgWRI1; direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positively associated with expression of LACS, DGAT, SAD and GPAT unigenes; directly binds TgDGAT1 promoter and enhances its transcription together with TgWRI1
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TgDGAT1; positively correlated LACS unigenes; DGAT unigenes; SAD unigenes; GPAT unigene
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong positive regulator of oil accumulation; natural coding variant alters magnitude of effect on oil content
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in leaves, arils, and developing kernels before full maturity; expression rises during kernel development and peaks in September; significantly correlated with oil content
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed transgenically in Arabidopsis; transiently expressed in tobacco leaves
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functional cooperation with TgWRI1 for TgDGAT1 promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Possibly gibberellin-responsive based on Arabidopsis LBD40/LBD41 discussion
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TRAS association; qRT-PCR expression profiling; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; stable overexpression in Arabidopsis; complementation of atlbd41 mutant; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A nonsynonymous SNP in TgLBD40 was significantly associated with kernel oil content. 2. TgLBD40 is highly expressed in oil-accumulating tissues and localizes to the nucleus. 3. Transient expression in tobacco and overexpression in Arabidopsis increased oil content, with the Ser allele stronger than the Ala allele. 4. TgLBD40 directly binds the TgDGAT1 promoter. 5. TgLBD40 requires TgWRI1 to enhance TgDGAT1 transcription, explaining its positive effect on oil accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for marker-assisted selection of favorable Ser allele and for transgenic/editing strategies to increase seed oil; stacking with TgWRI1 and DGAT1 may further enhance oil accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for overexpression/GFP fusion; TgDGAT1 native promoter in reporter assay
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The causal SNP at position 220,350 causes Ser-to-Ala substitution; TgLBD40-Ser showed stronger oil-promoting activity than TgLBD40-Ala. Complementation of Arabidopsis atlbd41 supports functional conservation.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio SMRT transcriptome and Illumina RNA-seq used for TRAS; supplementary data online, accession not provided

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2 family; two AP2 domains; conserved 14-3-3 binding motif
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts with TgLBD40 to activate TgDGAT1 promoter, supporting oil biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Oil biosynthesis / TAG biosynthetic gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; cooperative activation with TgLBD40
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates TgDGAT1 promoter only in combination with TgLBD40 under assay conditions
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Known key transcription factor in plant oil biosynthesis; here functions as required co-activator with TgLBD40 for TgDGAT1 activation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assayed by transient co-transformation in tobacco leaves
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functional cooperation with TgLBD40 for promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-luciferase assay; phylogenetic and sequence conservation analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TgWRI1 alone did not activate the TgDGAT1 promoter in the assay. 2. Co-expression of TgWRI1 with TgLBD40 strongly activated the TgDGAT1 promoter. 3. The weaker activation by TgLBD40-Ala plus TgWRI1 supports the functional importance of the TgLBD40 natural variant.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful stacking partner with TgLBD40 and TgDGAT1 to enhance oil biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a TRAS-identified association hit; included because it is experimentally central to the validated regulatory mechanism of the core gene TgLBD40.
